seoTitle: Spence vs Spring Gully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Spence and Spring Gully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Spence vs Spring Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Spence and Spring Gully.
heading: Spence vs Spring Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Spence, South Australia and Spring Gully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Spring Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Spence (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Spence or Spring Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Spence has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Spring Gully scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Spence scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Spring Gully scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Spence or Spring Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Spring Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
